channel.title: The Helping Hand
channel.description: Evidence-based parenting guidance for families at every stage.

item.title: Your Face Is Their Favourite Toy: The Science of the Infant Gaze
item.description:
Walk into any modern nursery and you'll find geometric mobiles and high-contrast flashcards. Cognitive science tells us your baby is looking for something entirely different — and it's already in the room.

Walk into any modern nursery, and you will likely find a sea of beautifully designed wooden toys, monochrome geometric mobiles, and high-contrast flashcards. While these look fantastic on an Instagram feed, cognitive science tells us that your baby is actually looking for something entirely different, completely free, and already in the room: your face. Researchers at the Birkbeck Babylab (Centre ...

Read the full article →

item.title: New Dads: Start the Bonding Now — Before Birth
item.description:
Early bonding doesn't have to wait until birth — in fact, the research suggests it should start in the womb. Evidence shows that babies can recognise their father's voice before they're even born.

Early bonding doesn't have to wait until birth — in fact, the research suggests it should start in the womb. This might sound surprising, but the science is clear: your baby is listening long before they arrive. Evidence shows that in the womb, babies can recognise voices they've heard regularly. A 2013 study published in the journal Acta Paediatrica found that newborns showed measurable responses...

Read the full article →

item.title: Why Your Toddler's Tantrums Are a Sign of Good Development
item.description:
The neuroscience of emotional regulation — and practical strategies that actually work when you're in the thick of it.

The prefrontal cortex — the part of the brain responsible for emotional regulation — is not fully developed until the mid-twenties. Your toddler is not being manipulative. They literally cannot regulate big emotions yet. Dr Daniel Siegel calls this 'flipping the lid' — when the emotional brain overwhelms the thinking brain entirely. Co-regulation (your calm presence) is what helps them come back d...

Read the full article →
